title: Boosting <mark>ionic conductivity</mark> of single-ion conductive polyelectrolyte elastomers via high-dielectric plasticizers
abstract: Published online: 03 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02604-8 Polyelectrolyte elastomers support selective and leakage-free ionic transport, but suffer from low <mark>ionic conductivity</mark>. A solid-state dielectric additive is introduced to various polyelectrolyte elastomers, which increases <mark>ionic conductivity</mark> by two orders of magnitude and maintains elasticity and leakage-free properties.

title: Bypassing the VTA: Thalamic modulation of striatal dopamine prioritizes <mark>safety</mark>
abstract: Published online: 04 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-73905-4 Avoiding danger even when competing goals are imminent supports survival, yet the mechanisms orchestrating this behavioral override remain unclear. Macdonald and colleagues1 describe a thalamostriatal microcircuit that modulates terminal dopamine release, bypassing canonical midbrain dopamine circuitry to prioritize <mark>safety</mark>.

title: Activating lattice oxygen in metal oxyhydroxides as durable electrodes for industrial-scale seawater splitting
abstract: Published online: 04 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-73894-4 Electrocatalytic seawater splitting enables green hydrogen production but is limited by corrosion and competing chlorine evolution. Here, the authors report a boron-doped iron-cobalt oxyhydroxide catalyst achieving high current density at low <mark>overpotential</mark> with good stability.

title: Electrode design and interface engineering of MXenes promote energy storage applications
abstract: Published online: 04 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s43246-026-01204-y This Review summarizes strategies for constructing composites based on MXenes for addressing challenges MXenes face in energy storage applications, such as ion deposition, shuttle effect, solid-electrolyte <mark>interphase</mark> formation, and contact resistance.

title: Sensor Driven Long Short-Term Memory Gate Recurrent Estimation with Deep Reinforcement Learning Based Charge Control for Battery Swapping Station Application
authors: Arun Chendhuran R, Senthil Kumar J and Sivaraju S. S.
abstract: Electric vehicle battery swapping technology can promote the high-quality development of new energy vehicles. Rapid analysis of the state of charge (SOC) is particularly essential for electric vehicle battery swapping. Traditional SOC assessment methods vary in model types and simplification techniques, often leading to skewed SOC estimation and unfair battery-swapping transactions in electric vehicles. To overcome this issue, this paper proposes an integrated method that accurately estimates SOC and controls charging current according to environmental conditions using an LSTM-based Gated Recu…

title: Electrochemical Performance, Degradation Mechanisms, and Thermal Effects in Starting Batteries: A Comprehensive Review
authors: Talal F. Qahtan, Hussein K. Amusa, Idris K. Popoola and Satam Alotibi
abstract: Starting batteries are critical electrochemical energy storage systems that deliver high power pulses for engine ignition and auxiliary electrical functions. Their performance, durability, and safety are governed by complex electrochemical interactions involving electrode materials, electrolytes, interfacial processes, and operating conditions. This review provides a comprehensive electrochemical perspective on major starting battery technologies, including flooded lead–acid, absorbed glass mat, gel cell, lithium-ion, and nickel–cadmium batteries. Key electrochemical performance-limiting facto…

title: Interpretable Battery SOH Prediction: A Comparative Interpretability Framework for Multi-Architecture ML Models
abstract: This work introduces a unified interpretability-efficiency framework for lithium-ion battery state of health (SOH) prediction using hybrid deep learning architectures. We comparatively analyze four hybrid models: CNN LSTM MultiHead, CNN Feature Extractor LSTM, DNN LSTM, and DNN BiLSTM to disentangle how network topology, feature composition, and computational design influence both predictive fidelity and physical interpretability. By integrating Monte Carlo Shapley (MC Shapley), background occlusion SHAP (BoSHAP), and ablation analysis, we quantify the contribution and robustness of five elect…

title: Autonomous Classification and Prediction of Degradation Transitions in Lithium-Ion Batteries
abstract: Lithium-ion batteries (LIBs) often exhibit a two-stage degradation behavior, transitioning from gradual capacity fade to accelerated aging at the degradation knee point. Accurate and timely assessment of a battery’s degradation stage relative to this knee is critical for battery management. However, most existing degradation-stage classification and knee-proximity prediction approaches rely on multi-cycle historical data, high-dimensional time-series inputs, or complex deep learning architectures, and are typically evaluated under a single knee definition, limiting interpretability and practic…
